channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: IAF’s Rudram-2 Anti-Radiation Missile Enters Final Validation, Production Likely by 2027
item.description: India’s indigenous Rudram-2 air-to-surface missile is approaching a critical milestone, with the system now in the final stages of validation trials and expected to receive production clearance in the near term. Developed by DRDO as a next-generation anti-radiation missile, Rudram-2 is designed to significantly enhance the Indian Air Force’s Suppression of Enemy Air Defences (SEAD) capability while replacing the ageing Russian-origin Kh-31 missile family.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Air Force Seeks Advanced Computer-Aided Wargaming Simulator
item.description: India is accelerating its transition toward technology-driven military training as the Ministry of Defence, through the Department of Military Affairs, has issued a Request for Proposal (RFP) for the procurement of a Computer-Aided Wargaming Simulator. The tender, being processed by the Indian Air Force, signals a growing emphasis on simulation-based strategic planning and operational preparedness.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 19 vs 2 : How 18 HQ-9B Interceptors Failed to Stop two Indian BrahMos-A ALCM in Operation Sindoor
item.description: A new video emerging from the final phases of Operation Sindoor has reignited discussions on modern aerial warfare, highlighting the remarkable performance of India’s air-launched BrahMos-A supersonic cruise missiles against one of Pakistan’s most advanced air defense systems.On May 10, 2025, during the heightened phase of the India-Pakistan conflict, the Indian Air Force deployed Su-30MKI fighters to launch two BrahMos-A missiles targeting Nur Khan Airbase in Rawalpindi.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: MKU Unveils NMK-3000 Upgrade Kits: Modernizing India’s AK-47 Assault Rifles for Enhanced Combat Effectiveness
item.description: Kanpur-based defence technology leader MKU has introduced the NMK-3000 series, a comprehensive upgrade package designed specifically to transform legacy AK-47 assault rifles into highly capable modern platforms. This development aligns with India’s push for indigenous solutions that extend the service life of existing inventories while incorporating advanced features for contemporary battlefield requirements.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Adani Defence Introduces Fibre Optic Drones: Revolutionizing India’s Unmanned Warfare Capabilities
item.description: Adani Defence and Aerospace has taken a significant step forward in India’s drone technology landscape by developing fibre optic drones, aligning with global trends that emphasize unjammable and resilient unmanned systems. This innovation draws from lessons in contemporary conflicts where traditional radio-controlled drones face severe disruptions from electronic warfare, prompting a shift toward fibre-optic guidance for enhanced reliability and stealth.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: J-35 Delivery to Pakistan Deferred Until After 2032 Amid Changing China-India-Pakistan Dynamics
item.description: In a significant development reshaping South Asian military dynamics, China has reportedly informed Pakistan that it will not deliver any J-35 fifth-generation stealth fighters to the Pakistan Air Force before 2032. This postponement comes against the backdrop of steadily improving relations between Beijing and New Delhi, coupled with a noticeable deterioration in high-level ties between Chinese and Pakistani leadership over the past year.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Military Roadmap Embraces AI Warfare, Drone Swarms and Killer Robots
item.description: India’s evolving military doctrine is entering a transformative phase, with artificial intelligence, drone swarms, cyber warfare, and autonomous combat systems now forming the core of its future warfighting strategy. A newly outlined tri-services AI policy underscores a decisive shift from conventional platform-centric warfare toward algorithm-driven, data-centric combat operations.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF’s Garud Special Forces to get compact UAV system with enhanced range, endurance
item.description: The government has started the process for procurement of a compact unmanned aerial vehicle system for the elite Special Forces unit of the IAF that can operate in extreme environmental conditions, and at altitudes up to 16,400 feet above mean sea level, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India approves $1.4 billion maritime insurance pool, hikes inflation-linked allowances
item.description: India has approved a 129.8-billion-rupee ($1.4 billion) guarantee for a maritime insurance pool, a minister said on Saturday, as wars and ?sanctions prompt insurers to withdraw cover, threatening trade flows.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Rajnath Singh to visit Germany next week to discuss strengthening defence cooperation
item.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh will travel to Germany for an official visit from April 21-23 to further strengthen the defence partnership between the two nations. During his visit, Rajnath Singh is set to hold talks with his German counterpart, Boris Pistorius, and other senior leaders of the government.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Iran stopped over 14 India-bound ships in Hormuz, hit one by gunfire
item.description: A convoy of 14 India-bound ships carrying crude oil and gas were stopped by the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) by firing at two of them while they were transiting the Strait of Hormuz, leading to 13 of the vessels returning to different locations in the Persian Gulf, official sources privy to the development said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Govt fixes map showing Arunachal Pradesh’s Pasighat as Chinese territory
item.description: The Registrar General of India on Saturday said that the “issue” related to a self-enumeration map showing Pasighat in the East Siang District of Arunachal Pradesh as a Chinese town ‘Medog’ has been resolved.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 2 US nationals were detailed at Srinagar airport today due to possession of Satellite Phone.
item.description: Two US nationals were detained at Srinagar International Airport on April 19, 2026, after security personnel discovered a satellite phone in their possession. One of the individuals has been identified as Jeffrey Scott Prather, a resident of Montana, USA.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Security tightened across Kashmir ahead of Pahalgam attack anniversary
item.description: Security has been beefed up at tourist spots across Kashmir ahead of the first anniversary of the Pahalgam terror attack in which 26 people were shot dead by Lashkar-e-Taiba terrorists on April 22 last year, officials said here.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: S. Korean President Departs For India, Summit Talks With Pm Modi On Monday
item.description: President Lee Jae Myung departed for India on Sunday for a two-nation trip that also includes a visit to Vietnam for summit talks, likely to focus on stabilising supply chains amid global energy market uncertainties stemming from the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: admin
